user_query:
I’m organizing our rap crew’s session notes and I want a small, local test-and-validate workflow around extracting action items from our notes. Please do the following using only the files under input/ and produce all outputs under output/:

1) Extract action items from each input/session_notes_YYYY-MM-DD.txt into JSONL under output/extracted/ with the same date in the filename (e.g., output/extracted/session_2026-04-10.jsonl). Recognize action items only on lines that start with "- [ ]" (open) or "- [x]" (completed) and use the pattern: free-text task followed by "(owner: <Name>; due: YYYY-MM-DD)". Each JSONL line must contain fields: task (string), owner (string), due (YYYY-MM-DD), completed (boolean). Do not include non-action lines.

2) Write a local validation step (it can be a simple script) that compares each extracted JSONL to its corresponding expected JSONL under input/ (same date). Create output/validation_report.json summarizing, for each session date: pass/fail, counts of matched items, missing items, unexpected items, and field-level mismatches (if any). Use exact string and date equality for matching; treat identity by the trio (task, owner, due).

3) Compute roll-up statistics across all extracted sessions, with de-duplication across sessions by task text: if the same exact task text appears in multiple sessions, keep only the most recent occurrence based on the session date in the filename (YYYY-MM-DD), along with its latest owner, due, and completed status. Save results to output/action_item_stats.json with:
- total_items, completed_items, open_items (integers)
- items_per_owner: { owner: open_item_count } (map; count only open items)
- open_items_due_within_7_days: an array of {task, owner, due} containing open items with due dates between anchor_date and anchor_date+7 days inclusive (anchor_date is provided in input/config.yaml)
- owners_with_most_open_items: array of owner name(s) tied for the highest open count

4) Prepare next session notes as output/next_meeting_notes.md using next_meeting_date from input/config.yaml. Include:
- A title with the date (e.g., "Next Session — 2026-04-18")
- A brief recap section that lists total_items, open_items, and completed_items from output/action_item_stats.json
- A section "Open action items grouped by owner" with open items grouped by owner and sorted by due date ascending; display each item as "- [ ] <task> (owner: <Owner>; due: <YYYY-MM-DD>)"
- A "Follow-ups" subsection listing any open items past due relative to anchor_date (due < anchor_date), using the same bullet format

Constraints:
- Use only the local input files; no Internet.
- Do not modify files under input/.
- Overwrite any existing files under output/.
- The validation report should pass when the extractor logic matches the provided expectations.

Input paths: see input/ files below. Required outputs: output/extracted/session_2026-04-10.jsonl, output/extracted/session_2026-04-14.jsonl, output/validation_report.json, output/action_item_stats.json, output/next_meeting_notes.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
